page: Microsoft Azure D8_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance belonging to the Dv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 4 physical Intel Xeon 8272CL cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a base clock speed of 2.6 GHz. The processor architecture is x86_64, supported by a 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and a 35 MB L3 cache. It includes advanced instruction sets such as AVX, AVX-512 Foundation, and AVX-512 VNNI, alongside hardware-accelerated cryptography via AES.

The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include any local storage or bundled GPUs, meaning all storage requirements must be met via external network-attached volumes. Additionally, the instance does not come with complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark evaluations position the D8_v4 in the average tier for most operations. Single-core and multi-core CPU performance tests, such as stress-ng and Geekbench, consistently fall within the middle 50% of tested systems. Memory bandwidth is measured at 415.35 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 49.51 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. The system exhibits weaker performance in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and Passmark physics simulations, placing in the bottom 25%. However, it maintains average throughput for static web serving at 7.21 GB/sec and Redis SET operations at 7.16 million operations per second. This profile makes the instance suitable for balanced workloads such as general-purpose web serving, database operations, and software compilation.
description: Microsoft Azure D8_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance from the Dv4 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it provides 8 dedicated vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM, maintaining a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio. The instance does not feature local storage or integrated GPUs, requiring network-attached storage for data persistence. Performance benchmarks place the D8_v4 in the average tier for most tasks, including Geekbench multi-core tests, static web serving at 7.21 GB/sec, and Redis SET operations at 7.16 million operations per second. It shows weaker performance in multi-threaded decompression and physics simulations. This resource configuration is qualitatively cost-efficient for standard enterprise workloads, making it well-suited for general-purpose web applications, software compilation, and moderate database operations.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D8_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB of RAM, and no local storage, delivering balanced performance for standard enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D8_v4 is a general-purpose VM with 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and no local storage, optimized for balanced workloads.
tagline: A balanced virtual machine offering dedicated Intel Xeon processing and proportional memory for standard enterprise applications and web serving.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8272CL processor at 2.6 GHz
32.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators included
Average performance in web serving, Redis operations, and software compilation
Best suited for general-purpose web applications and moderate database workloads
